title: Star Wars: Starfighter: Crossbones
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp|italics=1}} {{ComicSeries |image=[[File:StarfighterCrossbones1-CoverArt-EC.png]] |title=''Star Wars: Starfighter: Crossbones'' |writer=[[W. Haden Blackman|Haden Blackman]] |penciller=[[Ramón F. Bachs|Ramón Bachs]] |inker=[[Raul Fernandez]] |letterer=[[Steve Dutro]] |colorist=[[Brad Anderson]] |cover artist=[[J.H. Williams]], [[Mick Gray]], [[Jeremy Cox]] |editor=[[David Land|Dave Land]] |publisher=[[Dark Horse Comics]] |start date=[[January 9]], [[2002]]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54558/star_wars_starfighter_-_crossbones_2002_1|text=Star Wars: Starfighter - Crossbones (2002) #1}} |end date=[[March 13]], [[2002]]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54560/star_wars_starfighter_-_crossbones_2002_3|text=Star Wars: Starfighter - Crossbones (2002) #3}} |schedule=Monthly |format=Completed |issues=3 |timeline=[[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]]The date of this story is established internally in relation to {{Film|IV}}, which ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. |series= |preceded by= |followed by= }} '''''Star Wars: Starfighter: Crossbones''''' was a three-issue series of comics published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] between [[January 9]], [[2002]] and [[March 13]], [[2002]]. It is set in [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]]. This spin-off was based on the successful video game [[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']]. This series has not been collected as a trade paperback, but was eventually collected in ''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Menace Revealed]]''. It tells the story of the pirate captain [[Nym/Legends|Nym]] and his hunt of his nemesis [[Sol Sixxa]] on the planet [[Maramere]]. ==Development== ''Star Wars Starfighter: Crossbones'' was supposed to include only alien characters; author [[Ramón F. Bachs]] was explicitly asked not to include any [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. However, he convinced the rest of the team to include one Human as a background character in the cantina. That Human was a caricature of Star Wars fan [[User: Farlstendoiro|Skippy Farlstendoiro]], who has met Bachs many times usually disguised as a [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]]. As such, "Farlstendoiro" appeared next to a Mon Calamari. Furthermore, another human appeared in the first issue: [[Vana Sage]] (in a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]]). ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star Wars: Starfighter: Crossbones}} ===Issues=== {| {{Prettytable}} ! colspan="3" |Color code key: |- |style="background-color:#FFBB60;" width="24%" align="center"|Collected (TPB) issue |style="background-color:#40CC40;" width="24%" align="center"|Released issue |style="background-color:#FFFF80;" width="24%" align="center"|Future issue |} {| {{Prettytable}} !
